banner.scenario: This is a scenario explorer, not a forecast.
banner.caveat: The final curve does not depend on the order you move the sliders, but each measure's visible contribution depends on ordering, and the curve does not capture cross-effects between measures (e.g. CCUS and clinker reduction may double-count). In addition, some measures require expert review, and each measure maps differently onto concrete projects. Even so, the curve gives a general sense of the carbon-price levels needed to trigger real measures and projects in the economy, and offers benchmarks for the country's effective participation in international cooperation under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement.

howToRead.title: How to read a MACC
howToRead.width: Bar width = the amount of abatement (Mt CO₂/yr).
howToRead.height: Bar height = the unit cost of abatement (USD per tonne CO₂).
howToRead.below: Bars below zero pay for themselves — these are no-regrets measures.

assumptions.title: Global assumptions
assumptions.hint: Move the sliders — the curve recomputes in your browser and re-sorts on release.
